description: Session 16. Where canonical design systems give conflicting guidance + how ContentRX resolves. Feeds Session 35's `influences` sub-field work on standards.

entries.topic: What label should sit on a destructive-confirmation button?

positions.position: Use a specific verb naming what happens — 'Delete', 'Discard', 'Remove'. Avoid generic OK/Cancel on destructive alerts.
Specific verbs preferred; OK/Cancel is acceptable only when the question above the buttons is unambiguous.
Specific verb + the object: 'Delete order', 'Discard changes'.

entries.contentrx_resolution: Specific verbs, always. ACT-01 fires on generic button labels in destructive_action. CLR-01 reinforces this for the action-object specificity.

entries.topic: Active or passive voice in confirmation messages?

positions.position: Active voice by default — 'You saved the file.'
Passive acceptable in confirmations — the state (not the actor) is the focus. 'Changes saved.'
Passive acceptable; brevity beats voice consistency in confirmation moments.

entries.contentrx_resolution: VT-01 (active voice) relaxes in `confirmation` moment via moment weights. Passive is acceptable when the subject is the outcome, not the actor.

entries.topic: Sentence case or title case for headings?

positions.position: Sentence case for most UI text; title case only for specific navigation labels in iOS.
Sentence case — 'more casual and easier to read'.
Sentence case for all UI and docs.
Title case traditionally preferred for prose headings (not applied to UI).

entries.contentrx_resolution: Sentence case. CON-02 enforces it; title case is a style-guide holdover that reads formal in modern product UI.

entries.topic: When (if ever) are exclamation marks appropriate?

positions.position: Earned in celebration; avoid in error or neutral states.
Use only in true celebration — onboarding wins, milestones.
Rare; reserve for genuine enthusiasm, not standard confirmations.

entries.contentrx_resolution: Moment-weighted. GRM-03 suppresses in error_recovery, relaxes in celebration + first_encounter. The systems agree on the axis; they differ only on how liberally to allow exclamations in welcome moments.

entries.topic: What's the right tone for an empty state?

positions.position: Encouraging + action-oriented. Name what's missing, point at what to do.
Empty states are not blanks — they're opportunities. Explain, invite, maybe add personality.
Direct and helpful; avoid performative cheerfulness.

entries.contentrx_resolution: Empty_state moment weights push VT-05 (empathy) up and PRF-11 (dismissive language) down. The systems disagree on how much warmth is appropriate; ContentRX defers to the product's brand voice on warmth level but requires actionability.
